title: Oil back above $110 after expletive-laden Trump threat to Iran
description: The US president said Iran's bridges and power plants would be destroyed unless it opened the Strait of Hormuz.
content:
Global oil prices have risen back above $110 (£83.38) a barrel after US President Donald Trump threatened to destroy critical infrastructure in Iran unless it allows ships to cross the Strait of Hormuz.
On Monday morning in Asia, Brent crude rose by ... [2598 chars]

title: Trump news at a glance: president accused of ‘threatening possible war crimes’ in Iran post
description: Chuck Schumer accuses president of ‘ranting like unhinged madman’ in threat to obliterate Iran’s power plants and bridges. Key US politics stories from 5 April
content:
Donald Trump has faced sharp criticism after threatening to wipe out Iran’s power plants and bridges in an expletive-riddled social media post on Sunday.
The US president told Iran: “Open the Fuckin’ Strait, you crazy bastards, or you’ll be living in... [525 chars]

title: 'Benefits changes mean my son could lose out on £200 a month'
description: Charities say families are facing financial worries as changes to disability benefits come into effect.
content:
Erika Lye says she's "the sunshine" in her house, always smiling for her sons Logan, 20, and Jack, 16.
But behind closed doors, she is terrified about money.
She fears that a new change to the health element of Universal Credit could send her family ... [2896 chars]

title: Artemis II: The 40 minutes when the astronauts loses contact with Earth
description: As the astronauts pass behind the Moon they will experience a moment of silence and solitude as communication with the Earth is blocked.
content:
No-one will have been further from home than the Artemis astronauts.
But as the Earth shrinks ever smaller in their rear-view mirror, they've had a constant connection with mission control in Houston, Texas. The calm words from the Nasa team have giv... [3410 chars]

title: Blue badge permits now held by 1 in 15 adults in England
description: Councils urged to crack down on misuse of parking permits that help people with disabilities and health conditions
content:
Councils in England have been urged to crack down on the misuse of blue badge parking permits – legitimate and counterfeit – as the proportion of people holding them has reached one in 15.
The AA called for more to be done to detect offences such as ... [3109 chars]
